title: Send confirmation pop-up to the user when status changes
excerpt: Good afternoon, I’m trying to create an application using a node.js integration in which when the status changes, it sends a confirmation message to the user using monday.execute , for example. However, I’m struggling to…

posts.author: franLuna (Francisco Luna Quesada)
posts.cookedHtml: Good afternoon, I’m trying to create an application using a node.js integration in which when the status changes, it sends a confirmation message to the user using monday.execute , for example. However, I’m struggling to establish the connection and would appreciate any help. Thanks, everyone.

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello there @franLuna and welcome to the community!

I hope you like it here

If you are trying to use something like monday.execute(“notice”), that can not be used from the backend of a workflow (integration) feature. It can be used in views such as a board view or item view.

Was that the case?

Looking forward to hearing from you

Cheers,
Matias

posts.author: franLuna (Francisco Luna Quesada)
posts.cookedHtml:
Good morning! Thank you so much.
Yes, I am doing the warning from a workflow, but I made a view using a library, rendering it with express and putting the endpoint that I had configured in node.js with a get request, but it does not perform any action… so I understand that it is not possible to do it, right?

posts.author: rob (Roberto Tremonti)
posts.cookedHtml:
Matias:

that can not be used from the backend of a workflow (integration) feature

Hey @Matias.Monday
Any suggestions about sending a temporary notification to the user from the backend without using an update?

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @rob ,

I don’t think you have a way of sending a temporary one. Just a regular one.

posts.author: ianhunter373 (IH)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there an option now to send the monday.execute(“notice”) alert to monday, from the backend using a query? @Matias.Monday

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello there @ianhunter373,

That is not possible at the moment.

Let me know if you have any other questions!

Cheers,
Matias
